sections.label: Section 2.170
sections.original: πήματα · νοσεῖ δέ μοι πρόπας στόλος , οὐδʼ ἔνι φροντίδος ἔγχος
sections.translation: A plague is on all our people , and thought can find no weapon for defense . The fruits of the glorious earth do not grow ; by no birth of offspring do women surmount the pangs in which they shriek .

sections.label: Section 2.175
sections.original: ἄλλον δʼ ἂν ἄλλῳ προσίδοις ἅπερ εὔπτερον ὄρνιν
sections.translation: You can see life after life speed away , like a bird on the wing , swifter than irresistible fire , to the shore of the western god .

sections.label: Section 2.179
sections.original: ὧν πόλις ἀνάριθμος ὄλλυται ·
sections.translation: With such deaths , past numbering , the city perishes .

sections.label: Section 2.180
sections.original: νηλέα δὲ γένεθλα πρὸς πέδῳ θαναταφόρα κεῖται ἀνοίκτως ·
sections.translation: Unpitied , her children lie on the ground , spreading pestilence , with no one to mourn them . Meanwhile young wives and grey-haired mothers raise a wail at the steps of the altars , some here , some there ,

sections.label: Section 2.185
sections.original: παιὰν δὲ λάμπει στονόεσσά τε γῆρυς ὅμαυλος
sections.translation: and groan in supplication for their terrible woes . The prayers to the Healer ring clear , and with them the voice of lamentation . For which things , golden daughter of Zeus , send us the bright face of comfort .

sections.label: Section 3.190
sections.original: Ἄρεά τε τὸν μαλερόν , ὃς νῦν ἄχαλκος ἀσπίδων
sections.translation: Grant that the fierce god of death , who now without the bronze of shields , though among cries like those of battle , wraps me in the flames of his onset , may turn his back in speedy flight from our land , borne by a favorable wind to the great chamber of Amphitrite ,

sections.label: Section 3.195
sections.original: εἴτʼ ἐς τὸν ἀπόξενον ὅρμων
sections.translation: or to the Thracian waves , those waters where none find haven . For if night leaves anything undone in the working of destruction , day follows to accomplish it . You who wield the

sections.label: Section 3.200
sections.original: ἀστραπᾶν κράτη νέμων ,
sections.translation: powers of fiery lightning , Zeus our father , slay him beneath your thunder-bolt .

sections.label: Section 3.203
sections.original: Λύκειʼ ἄναξ , τά τε σὰ χρυσοστρόφων ἀπʼ ἀγκυλᾶν
sections.translation: Lycean Lord , would that the shafts from your bent bow ’ s string of woven gold might

sections.label: Section 3.205
sections.original: ἀρωγὰ προσταχθέντα τάς τε πυρφόρους
sections.translation: go forth in their might , our champions in the face of the foe , and the flashing fires of Artemis too , with which she darts through the Lycian hills . I call him whose locks are bound with gold ,

sections.label: Section 3.210
sections.original: τᾶσδʼ ἐπώνυμον γᾶς ,
sections.translation: who is named with the name of this land , ruddy Bacchus to whom Bacchants cry , to draw near with the blaze of his shining torch ,

sections.label: Section 3.215
sections.original: πεύκᾳ ʼπὶ τὸν ἀπότιμον ἐν θεοῖς θεόν .
sections.translation: our ally against the god unhonored among the gods .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: αἰτεῖς · ἃ δʼ αἰτεῖς , τἄμʼ ἐὰν θέλῃς ἔπη
sections.translation: You pray . And in answer to your prayer , if you will give a loyal reception to my words and minister to your own disease , you may hope to find help and relief from woes . These words I will speak publicly , as one who was a stranger to the report ,

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: ξένος δὲ τοῦ πραχθέντος · οὐ γὰρ ἂν μακρὰν
sections.translation: a stranger to the deed . I would not go far on the trail if I were tracing it alone , without a clue . But as it is — since it was only after the event that I was counted a Theban among Thebans — to you , Cadmeans all , I do thus proclaim : Whoever knows

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: κάτοιδεν ἀνδρὸς ἐκ τίνος διώλετο ,
sections.translation: by whom Laius son of Labdacus was slain , I order him to declare all to me . And if he is afraid , I order him to remove the danger of indictment from his path by denouncing himself : he will suffer no other punishment , but will only leave this land , unhurt .

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: εἰ δʼ αὖ τις ἄλλον οἶδεν ἐξ ἄλλης χθονὸς
sections.translation: If anyone knows the assassin to be an alien , from another land , let him not keep silent . I will reward him , and my thanks shall rest with him besides . But if he keeps silent , if anyone , through fear , seeks to screen himself or a friend from my orders ,

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: ἃκ τῶνδε δράσω , ταῦτα χρὴ κλύειν ἐμοῦ .
sections.translation: then hear what I shall do : I charge you that no resident of this land , which I rule , give shelter to or address that murderer , whoever he is , or make him a partner in prayer or sacrifice ,

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: κοινὸν ποεῖσθαι , μήτε χέρνιβας νέμειν ·
sections.translation: or give him a share of the lustral rite . Ban him from your houses , all of you , knowing that this is the defilement , as the oracle of the Pythian god has recently shown to me . In this way

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: τῷ τʼ ἀνδρὶ τῷ θανόντι σύμμαχος πέλω ·
sections.translation: I am an ally both to the god and to the dead man . And I pray solemnly that the slayer , whoever he is , whether he alone is guilty or he has partners , may , in the horrible way he deserves , wear out his unblest life . And for myself I pray that if he should ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: ἐν τοῖς ἐμοῖς γένοιτʼ ἐμοῦ συνειδότος ,
sections.translation: with my knowledge , become a resident of my house , I may suffer the same things which I have just called down on others . And I order you to make all these words good , for my sake , for the sake of the god , and for the sake of our land , thus rendered unfruitful and ungodly .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: οὐδʼ εἰ γὰρ ἦν τὸ πρᾶγμα μὴ θεήλατον ,
sections.translation: For even if the matter had not been urged upon us by a god , it still would not have been fit that you should leave the guilt unpunished as it is , when one so noble — and he your king — had perished . You should have searched it out . But now , since I hold the powers which he once held ,

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: ἔχων δὲ λέκτρα καὶ γυναῖχʼ ὁμόσπορον ,
sections.translation: possessing his bed and the wife who bore his children , and since , had his hope of offspring not been unsuccessful , children born of one mother would have tied us with a common bond — as it was , fate swooped upon his head — I will uphold this cause , as though it were that of my own father ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: ὑπερμαχοῦμαι κἀπὶ πᾶν ἀφίξομαι ,
sections.translation:
and will leave no stone unturned in my search for the one who shed the blood , for the honor of the son of Labdacus and of Polydorus and the elder Cadmus and Agenor of old .
And for those who do not obey me , I pray that the gods

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: μήτʼ ἄροτον αὐτοῖς γῆς ἀνιέναι τινὰ
sections.translation: send them neither harvest of the earth nor fruit of the womb , but that they perish with the present fate , or one still worse . But to all you , the loyal Cadmeans who are satisfied by these things , may justice , our ally ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: χοἰ πάντες εὖ ξυνεῖεν εἰσαεὶ θεοί .
sections.translation: and all the gods be gracious always .

sections.label: Section 276
sections.original: ὥσπερ μʼ ἀραῖον ἔλαβες , ὧδʼ , ἄναξ , ἐρῶ .
sections.translation: As you have put me under oath , on my oath , my king , I will speak . I am not the slayer , nor can I reveal him . As for the investigation , it was for Phoebus , who enjoined it , to tell us who did the deed .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: δίκαιʼ ἔλεξας · ἀλλʼ ἀναγκάσαι θεοὺς
sections.translation: Justly said . But no man on earth can force the gods to do what they do not want .

sections.label: Section 282
sections.original: τὰ δεύτερʼ ἐκ τῶνδʼ ἂν λέγοιμʼ ἁμοὶ δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: I would like to say what seems to me the next best course .

sections.label: Section 283
sections.original: εἰ καὶ τρίτʼ ἐστί , μὴ παρῇς τὸ μὴ οὐ φράσαι .
sections.translation: And if there is a third course , do not hesitate to reveal it too .

sections.label: Section 284
sections.original: ἄνακτʼ ἄνακτι ταὔθʼ ὁρῶντʼ ἐπίσταμαι
sections.translation: I know that our lord Teiresias is the seer most like our

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: μάλιστα Φοίβῳ Τειρεσίαν , παρʼ οὗ τις ἂν
sections.translation: lord Apollo : from him , my king , an investigator might learn most clearly about these affairs .

sections.label: Section 287
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐν ἀργοῖς οὐδὲ τοῦτʼ ἐπραξάμην .
sections.translation: Not even this have I neglected . On Creon ’ s suggestion , I sent a man twice to bring him . And I have been wondering for some time why he is not here .

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τά γʼ ἄλλα κωφὰ καὶ παλαίʼ ἔπη .
sections.translation: Indeed — his skill apart — the rumors are but faint and old .

sections.label: Section 291
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα ταῦτα ; πάντα γὰρ σκοπῶ λόγον .
sections.translation: What are the rumors ? I am investigating every tale .

sections.label: Section 292
sections.original: θανεῖν ἐλέχθη πρός τινων ὁδοιπόρων .
sections.translation: He was said to have been killed by some wayfarers .

sections.label: Section 293
sections.original: ἤκουσα κἀγώ . τὸν δʼ ἰδόντʼ οὐδεὶς ὁρᾷ .
sections.translation: I too have heard that . But no one sees the one who saw it .

sections.label: Section 294
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴ τι μὲν δὴ δείματός γʼ ἔχει μέρος ,
sections.translation: If he knows what fear is , he will not hesitate to come forward when he

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: τὰς σὰς ἀκούων οὐ μενεῖ τοιάσδʼ ἀράς ,
sections.translation: hears your curses , so dire are they .

sections.label: Section 296
sections.original: ᾧ μή ʼστι δρῶντι τάρβος , οὐδʼ ἔπος φοβεῖ .
sections.translation: When a man does not shrink from a deed , he is not scared by a word .

sections.label: Section 297
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὑξελέγξων αὐτὸν ἔστιν · οἵδε γὰρ
sections.translation: But there is one to convict him . But here they bring at last the godlike prophet , the only man in whom truth lives .

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ὦ πάντα νωμῶν Τειρεσία , διδακτά τε
sections.translation: Teiresias , whose soul grasps all things , both that which may be told and that which is unspeakable , the Olympian secrets and the affairs of the earth , you feel , though you cannot see , what a huge plague haunts our state . From which , great prophet , we find you to be our protector and only savior .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: Φοῖβος γάρ , εἴ τι μὴ κλύεις τῶν ἀγγέλων ,
sections.translation: Now , Phoebus — if indeed you have not already heard the news — sent answer to our question that the only way to rid ourselves of this pest that afflicts us is to discover the slayers of Laius , and then to slay them or banish them from our land .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: σύ νυν φθονήσας μήτʼ ἀπʼ οἰωνῶν φάτιν
sections.translation: So do not begrudge us the voice of the birds or any other path of prophecy , but save yourself and your state , save me , save all that is defiled by the dead . We are in your hands , and man ’ s noblest task is to help others

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ἔχοι τε καὶ δύναιτο , κάλλιστος πόνων .
sections.translation: to the best of his means and powers .

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , φρονεῖν ὡς δεινὸν ἔνθα μὴ τέλη
sections.translation: Alas , how terrible it is to have wisdom when it does not benefit those who have it . I knew this well , but let it slip from my mind : otherwise I would not have come here .

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; ὡς ἄθυμος εἰσελήλυθας .
sections.translation: What now ? How disheartened you have come !

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: ἄφες μʼ ἐς οἴκους · ῥᾷστα γὰρ τὸ σόν τε σὺ
sections.translation: Let me go home . For you will bear your own burden to the end , and I will bear mine , if you consent .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἔννομʼ εἶπας οὔτε προσφιλῆ πόλει
sections.translation: Your words are strange and unkind to the state which nurtured you , since you withhold this response .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: ὁρῶ γὰρ οὐδὲ σοὶ τὸ σὸν φώνημʼ ἰὸν
sections.translation: I see that you , for your part , speak inappropriately .

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: πρὸς καιρόν · ὡς οὖν μηδʼ ἐγὼ ταὐτὸν πάθω —
sections.translation: Therefore do not speak , so I will not suffer the same .

sections.label: Section 326
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν φρονῶν γʼ ἀποστραφῇς , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: For the love of the gods , do not turn away , if you have knowledge : all we suppliants implore you on our knees .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: πάντες γὰρ οὐ φρονεῖτʼ · ἐγὼ δʼ οὐ μή ποτε
sections.translation: For all of you are without knowledge . But never will I reveal my troubles — not to call them yours .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: τί φής ; ξυνειδὼς οὐ φράσεις , ἀλλʼ ἐννοεῖς
sections.translation: What are you saying ? Do you know the secret and refuse to tell it ? Will you betray and destroy the state ?

sections.label: Section 332
sections.original: ἐγὼ οὔτʼ ἐμαυτὸν οὔτε σʼ ἀλγυνῶ . τί ταῦτʼ
sections.translation: I will grieve neither myself nor you . Why do you ask these things in vain ? You will not learn the answers from me .

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: οὐκ , ὦ κακῶν κάκιστε , καὶ γὰρ ἂν πέτρου
sections.translation: Will you not , basest of the base —

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: φύσιν σύ γʼ ὀργάνειας , ἐξερεῖς ποτε ,
sections.translation: you would anger a stone — speak out ? can nothing touch you ? Will you never make an end ?

sections.label: Section 337
sections.original: ὀργὴν ἐμέμψω τὴν ἐμήν , τὴν σὴν δʼ ὁμοῦ
sections.translation: You blame my anger , but do not perceive your own : no , you blame me .

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: τίς γὰρ τοιαῦτʼ ἂν οὐκ ἂν ὀργίζοιτʼ ἔπη
sections.translation: Who would not be angry hearing such words ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: κλύων , ἃ νῦν σὺ τήνδʼ ἀτιμάζεις πόλιν ;
sections.translation: with which you now are slighting the city ?

sections.label: Section 341
sections.original: ἥξει γὰρ αὐτά , κἂν ἐγὼ σιγῇ στέγω .
sections.translation: The future will come of itself , though I shroud it in silence .
